data.title: Al Kathiri Unit Lands SAR83 Million National Housing Order
data.description:
Al Kathiri Holding (SASE:3008) unit obtained a contract worth 82.7 million Saudi riyals from National Housing Co., or NHC, for electromechanical works at the Melaa project in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia.

Under the six-month contract, the construction materials company's subsidiary, Msandh Alemdad, will oversee the finishing of 295 residential units at the development, according to a Sunday release.

The financial impact of the project will be reflected from the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Electrical Industries Declares H2 2024 Dividend
data.description:
Electrical Industries (SASE:1303) on Sunday declared a cash dividend of 0.125 Saudi riyal per share for the second half of 2024.

The Saudi Arabia-listed electrical services company's dividend will be paid on April 13 to shareholders on record March 20. It paid 0.075 riyal per share a year ago.

data.title: Crypto Pundit Dumps Bitcoin Holdings Sub-$100,000, Lists Reasons Why It’s Time To Short
data.description:
Bitcoin has extended its decline below $90,000 as on-chain data shows whales selling off in massive amounts. This price decline comes amidst the otherwise bullish news of Donald Trump signing an executive order for a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve (SBR). The lack of bullish momentum despite this has brought into play the possibility of an extended bearish move from here. 

A well-known crypto analyst, Doctor Profit, has raised alarms about an impending major correction in Bitcoin’s price. In a detailed post on social media platform X, he outlined his reasons for this shift, arguing that the current market conditions signal the start of the first large Bitcoin correction of this cycle.

Strategic Bitcoin Reserve: A Misinterpreted Narrative?

Popular crypto analyst Doctor Profit revealed that he is selling a significant portion of his holdings and entering short positions. Notably, the analyst pointed to the recent news surrounding the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve as a key factor that led him to reevaluate his bullish stance. He emphasized that while retail investors see this as a game-changing development, large players and whale investors have already priced in the impact. 

Many crypto investors expected an accumulation of Bitcoin by the US government in order to strengthen the reserve. However, instead of the expected ensuing buying pressure on Bitcoin, the executive order focused on Bitcoin confiscated from previous seizures, which left bullish investors underwhelmed. 

According to Doctor Profit, the decision to sign off on this policy earlier than anticipated signaled a shift in market dynamics. His expectation was that this move would materialize months later, allowing Bitcoin’s price to sustain upward momentum before the first significant correction. Instead, he now sees this as the primer for a long-term decline.

Is This The Beginning Of Bitcoin’s First Big Correction? Price Levels To Warch

Doctor Profit firmly believes that Bitcoin has yet to experience a proper correction in this cycle, noting bull market trends where the asset has seen at least one 40-50% drop before reaching new all-time highs. He sees the recent developments as the final push before a 40% to 50% decline. As such, the analyst noted that this is the ideal window for distributing sell orders and entering short positions.

His outlook suggests a retracement to as low as $50,000–$60,000 before Bitcoin resumes its long-term bullish trajectory. Breaking down his trading strategy, he disclosed that he has already sold 50% of his Bitcoin holdings, which he accumulated at $16,000. He has placed short orders within the $90,000–$102,000 range, with target profits set at $74,000 for the first take-profit level, followed by a complete exit in the $50,000–$60,000 region and a full buyback to double holdings.

Despite his short-term bearish outlook, the analyst maintains that Bitcoin will eventually rally to new highs in the $120,000–$130,000 range. At the time of writing, Bitcoin is trading at $86,530.

data.title: Jahez Posts Rise in FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Jahez International Co. for Information Systems Technology (SASE:6017) said Sunday that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 increased year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 188 million Saudi riyals, compared with 125.3 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.92 riyal from 0.61 riyal.

The Saudi lifestyle technology platform operator's revenue was 2.22 billion riyals, compared with 1.78 billion riyals a year ago.

data.title: L'azurde Co. for Jewelry Posts Lower FY24 Profit; Revenue Up
data.description:
L'azurde Co. for Jewelry (SASE:4011) said Sunday that profit for full-year 2024 declined, while revenue rose year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 11.7 million Saudi riyals, compared with 30.8 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.2 riyal from 0.54 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed jewelry company's revenue was 2.11 billion riyals, compared with 1.86 billion riyals a year ago.

Meanwhile, its board proposed not to pay dividends for 2024. It paid 0.30 riyal per share a year ago.

data.title: Saudi's Edaa Adds Derayah Financial's Subscribed Securities to Holders Accounts
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's Securities Depository Center, or Edaa, said that the subscribed securities in Derayah Financial Co. (SASE:4084) were deposited to the accounts of eligible securities holders.

The subscribed securities of the digital investment platform operator were added on Sunday, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Mobi Industry's Dividend Distribution Policy Secures Board Approval
data.description:
Mobi Industry's (SASE:9517) board approved its new dividend distribution policy, according to a Sunday release.

The Saudi Arabia-based plastics, detergent, and chemical fertilizer manufacturer's policies include the timing and method of dividend payment, required approvals, and the procedure for unreceived dividends.

As part of the approved policies, dividends will be distributed to registered shareholders within 15 working days from maturity.

data.title: Derayah Financial to Begin Trading on Saudi Exchange
data.description: Shares of Saudi Arabia's independent digital investment platform Derayah Financial (SASE:4084) will list on the Saudi Exchange's main market on Monday, with trading to begin on the same day, the bourse said Sunday.

data.title: Fourth Milling Logs FY24 Climb in Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Fourth Milling (SASE:2286) said Sunday that profit and revenue for full-year 2024 increased year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 170.9 million Saudi riyals, compared with 142.8 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.32 riyal from 0.26 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed milling company's revenue was 629 million riyals, compared with 558.1 million riyals a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board declared a cash dividend of 0.11 riyal per share for the second half of 2024, payable within 15 working days to shareholders on record March 24.

data.title: Nadec Plans Share Buyback
data.description:
The board of National Agricultural Development (SASE:6010), d/b/a Nadec, resolved to buy back up to 1 million shares, representing 0.33% of the company's ordinary share capital, for allocation to the long-term employee incentive program.

If the proposal is approved at the extraordinary general assembly, the Saudi Arabia-listed agricultural company will repurchase the shares in the following 18 months.

The company will use internal resources to fund the buyback.

data.title: Amak Extends Deadline for Potential License Deal With UK's Power Metal Resources
data.description:
Al Masane Al Kobra Mining (SASE:1322), d/b/a Amak, deferred the signing of a potential license deal with British metals explorer Power Metal Resources by an additional three months to June 9, according to a Sunday release.

The companies in September 2024 agreed to enter into a binding deal wherein Power Metal will invest $3 million in exchange for a 49% stake in Amak's Qatan exploration license in Saudi Arabia.

data.title: Arabian Shield Cooperative Insurance Posts Higher FY24 Profit, Insurance Revenue
data.description:
Arabian Shield Cooperative Insurance (SASE:8070) said Sunday that profit and insurance revenue for full-year 2024 increased year over year.

Net profit after zakat attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 71 million Saudi riyals, compared with 66.9 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.89 riyal from 1.01 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed insurer's insurance revenue was 1.57 billion riyals, compared with 1.13 billion riyals a year ago.

data.title: Sumou Real Estate Posts Growth in FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Sumou Real Estate (SASE:4323) said Sunday profit and revenue for full-year 2024 jumped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 106.6 million Saudi riyals, compared with 103 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 2.13 riyals from 2.06 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-based real estate company's revenue was 429.5 million riyals, compared with 273.8 million riyals a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board declared a cash dividend of 0.5 riyal per share for the second half of 2024, payable on May 1 to shareholders on record April 17. It paid no dividend a year ago.

data.title: Saudi Co. for Hardware Posts Smaller Loss in FY24; Revenue Climbs
data.description:
Saudi Co. for Hardware (SASE:4008) on Sunday reported a narrower loss for full-year 2024, while revenue rose year over year.

Loss for the six months ended June 30 was 14.1 million Saudi riyals, compared with a loss of 69 million riyals earlier. Loss per share moved to 0.39 riyal from a loss per share of 1.92 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-based household and office supplies retailer's revenue was 993.6 million riyals, compared with 930.1 million riyals a year ago.

data.title: Market Chatter: Saudi Arabia's PIF Reportedly Earmarks $100 Million for AirAsia Investment
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, or PIF, will reportedly invest $100 million in Malaysian air carrier AirAsia to grow its relations with the country.

Bloomberg News reported on March 6, citing people familiar with the matter, that discussions are ongoing and there is no guarantee that a deal would materialize.

The sources added that the investment is part of AirAsia's $226 million fundraising, where it may offer up to 15% shareholdings to investors.

The PIF did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Michael Saylor pushes US gov’t to purchase up to 25% of Bitcoin supply
data.description:
Strategy founder Michael Saylor has proposed that the United States government aims to acquire up to 25% of Bitcoin’s total supply over the next decade for its Strategic Bitcoin Reserve.

“Acquire 5-25% of the Bitcoin network in trust for the nation through consistent, programmatic daily purchases between 2025 and 2035, when 99% of all BTC will have been issued,” Saylor wrote in a document titled “A Digital Assets Strategy to Dominate the 21st Century Global Economy.”

Saylor reiterates to the US government, “Never sell your Bitcoin”

Saylor presented the document to US President Donald Trump, government executives, and global crypto leaders at the White House Crypto Summit on March 7.

He explained that the government should stick to a “Never sell your Bitcoin” policy, predicting that by 2045, the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve could generate over $10 trillion annually, and serve as a “perpetual source of prosperity” for Americans.

Up until 2045, Saylor said the Reserve could generate between $16 trillion and $81 trillion for the US Treasury, potentially easing the national debt.

Earlier that day, Trump signed an executive order establishing a “Strategic Bitcoin Reserve” and a “Digital Asset Stockpile,” initially funded with cryptocurrency seized in criminal cases.

While it didn’t include an immediate plan to buy more Bitcoin, the order stated that the Treasury and Commerce secretaries would develop “budget-neutral strategies” for buying more Bitcoin, ensuring no added costs for taxpayers.

25% supply allocation far exceeds previous proposals

If the government secured 25% of Bitcoin’s total supply, it would hold 5.25 million BTC — far more than the 1 million BTC (5% of the supply) proposed by Wyoming Senator Cynthia Lummis in the Bitcoin Act introduced in July 2024.

Meanwhile, Saylor has continued accumulating Bitcoin, purchasing an additional $2 billion worth on Feb. 24, bringing Strategy’s total holdings to nearly 500,000 BTC.

The acquisition came after Strategy raised another $2 billion in a senior convertible note offering to purchase more Bitcoin, the firm announced earlier on Feb. 24.

data.title: DBRS Upgrades Greece’s Ratings to BBB
data.description:
Morningstar DBRS changed Greece's credit rating to BBB from BBB (low), citing a healthier banking sector and the continued reduction in the country's general government debt ratio. "The upgrade reflects DBRS Morningstar’s view that legacy risks in the banking system have receded, along with continued overperformance in fiscal targets," DBRS said in a statement.

Greece expects GDP to grow by 2.3% in 2025, with a primary budget surplus of 2.4% of GDP, driven by higher investment and strong tourism revenue.

Meanwhile, the rating agency expects the country’s public debt-to-GDP ratio to fall below 14% in 2027.

Standard & Poor's credit rating for Greece stands at BBB- with a positive outlook.

Moody's credit rating for Greece was last set at Ba1 with a positive outlook.
